On 17 June 1944, the following ships arrived at Algiers after having been detached from the convoy; Badjestan, Chiswick, Empire Summer, Fort Pembina, Gylfe, Hardingham, Hoegh Hood, Iceland, Laurent Meeus, Ocean Rider, Samhope and HMS Colombo while the following ships joined the convoy coming from Algiers; Alex (British, 3932 GRT, built 1914), Belgian Trader (Belgian, 2890 GRT, built 1942), Cape Howe (British, 6999 GRT, built 1943), Clausina (British (tanker), 8083 GRT, built 1938), Empire Gareth (British, 2847 GRT, built 1942), Fort Anne (British, 7134 GRT, built 1943), Gouverneur General Lepine (French, 3509 GRT, built 1923) and Ville de Majunga (British (former French), 6174 GRT, built 1931).

On 18 June 1944, the following ships arrived at Oran after having been detached from the convoy; A.P. Hill , Andrew Briscoe, Cape Howe, Clydefield, Fort Anne, George Davis, James Moore, John Trumbull, Lincoln Steffens, Robert T. Hill and Tabitha Brown while the transport Sinnington Court (British, 6910 GRT, built 1928) joined the convoy coming from Oran.
The convoy arrived at Gibraltar on 20 June 1944.
15 Jun 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Malta to join convoy MKS 52.
[For more info on this convoy see the event ' Convoy MKS 52 ' for 8 June 1944.]

[As no log of this period is available so her exact movements are uncertain. She sailed to join convoy MKS 52 on passage from Port Said to Gibraltar. She had received orders to leave the convoy near Bone on the 16th and proceed to La Maddalena but it appears that she went to Bone instead, perhaps to remedy to some defects.] (90)
16 Jun 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Bone. (90)
18 Jun 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Bone for La Maddalena where she was to join the 10th submarine flotilla. (90)
20 Jun 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at La Maddalena. (90)
23 Jun 1944 At 1700 B/23, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed La Maddalena for her 24th war patrol (20th in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol off Southern France.
25 Jun 1944 At 0145 B/25, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) entered area 'ST' which was her patrol area. (27)
26 Jun 1944 At 0435 B/26, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) dived in position 43°05'N, 06°38'E and commenced to patrol at periscope depth 2 miles to the south of Cape Lardier.
At 2030 B/26, a destroyer, UJ-boat and a naval tug (A/S equipped) were seen proceeding eastwards from Bolnes roads.
The destroyer and UJ boat were most likely the German auxiliary submarine chasers UJ 6073 and UJ 6079.
At 2105 B/26, an F-lighter was seen close inshore, proceeding on course 070° at a speed of 8 knots. Lt. Kett came to a 100° track closing intending to fire two torpedoes.

At 2115 B/26, the attack was broken off as it was not possible to reduce the range below 4000 yards. The chances of hitting and F-lighter did not justify firing torpedoes or compromising the submarines position so early in the patrol.

At 2130 B/26, the destroyer and UJ-boat, on reaching Cape Lardier, turned round and proceeded towards Hyeres roads. Both vessels were zigzagging continuously. The F-lighter and A/S tug proceeded eastwards in company.
The tug was most likely the L'Obstiné (323 GRT, built 1921) and the brand new landing barge MFP 935.

They had sailed from Toulon for St. Tropez at 1715 B/26. They were to sail from St. Tropez at 2300 B/26 for Nice with the German transport Pallas (5260 GRT, built 1925) in tow escorted by the German auxiliary minesweepers M 6020, M 6021 and M 6027. They arrived at Nice at 0930 B/27.
At 2200 B/26, HMS Ultimatum surfaced in position 43°05'N, 06°40'E and pratrolled five miles to the south-east of Cape Camarat.
At 2310 B/26, the Capt. S.10's signal timed 1255 B/26 was received which granted HMS Ultimatum freedom of action in area's 'ST', 'SU' and 'SW'. (91)
28 Jun 1944 At 0432 B/28, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) dived south of Toulon Bay in position 42°59'N, 06°01'E and set course to close the Little Pass.
At 0900 B/28, two motor torpedo boats (or motor minesweepers) were seen patrolling inside Toulon Bay.
[We have been unable to identify these vessels.]
At 1945 B/28, a destroyer and an UJ boat were observed to leave Toulon and commence an A/S sweep. Both vessels were transmitting on Asdic and carried out a continuous zig-zag.
These were most likely the German auxiliary submarine chasers UJ 6073 and UJ 6079 which left Toulon at 1915 B/28 to hunt a reported submarine in the Nice-Cannes area.
At 2000 B/28, two former French Chasseurs joined the A/S sweep. These too were transmitting on Asdic.
At 2130 B/28, the destroyer and UJ boat proceeded to sweep westward eventually disappearing in the direction 6 miles south of Cape Sicie. The Chasseurs continued to patrol near Little Pass.
At 2200 B/28, it was getting too dark to see the enemy against the land so course was set to seaward.
At 2336 B/28, HMS Ultimatum surfaced in position 42°54'N, 06°02'E and commenced to patrol in the path of the moon while charging the battery. (91)
29 Jun 1944 At 0429 B/29, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) dived south of Toulon Bay in position 43°00'N, 06°01'E and set course to patrol off the Giens Peninsula / Little Pass area.

At 1230 B/29, an aircraft report was received which reported a 4000 tons vessel and a 2000 ton vessel in position 43°22'N, 06°59'E proceeding on course 240° at a speed of 9 knots. It was considered likely these ships could be expected to pass through Little Pass shortly after 1530 B/29. Most likely this were the ships reported by the aircraft.
At 1605 B/29, a destroyer and UJ boat were seen leaving the Little Pass and proceeding towards Toulon. Both vessels were transmitting on Asdic and carrying out a continuous zig-zag.
At 1800 B/29, the destroyer and UJ boat were seen to enter Toulon.
This were once again the German auxiliary submarine chasers UJ 6073 and UJ 6079 returning from A/S operations.
At 2110 B/29, four Chasseurs or motor torpedo boats / motor minesweepers were observed to be patrolling to the south of Cape Cepet.
At 2000 B/29, HMS Ultimatum retired to seaward, surfacing at 2314 B/29 in position 42°57'N, 06°00'E. The course was then set to patrol off Cape Camarat. (91)
3 Jul 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) fired two torpedoes at the German auxiliary submarine chaser UJ 6073 south of Cannes, southern France. The torpedoes however missed their target.
UJ 6073 was on an A/S patrol together with UJ 6079.
0059 B/3 - Sighted a white light flashing morse bearing 340°.

0104 B/3 - Two darkened ships were made out steaming on a course of 200° distance 2000 yards. As the vessels were at close range and were signalling, Lt. Kett thought he might be sighted. He turned his submarine stern on to watch events unfold.
0109 B/3 - The vessels were identified as a destroyer and a UJ boat, and as they showed no sign of taking action against Ultimatum Lt. Kett altered course to attack.

0114 B/3 - In position 43°24'N, 06°57'E fired four torpedoes. 40 seconds later one torpedo was seen to hit causing a column of water rise 50 feet into the air completely enveloping the target (this actually was not a hit, the torpedo exploded prematurely). Six seconds later a second torpedo was heard to explode but this was not observed as the submarine was now diving towards 150 feet. The target is thought to be destroyed.
1115 B/3 - Dived an took evasive action at 150 feet.
0120 B/3 - Faint A/S transmissions picked up astern while at 150 feet.
0123 B/3 - Heard two loud explosion at six second intervals which were considered to be torpedoes exploding against the land.
0200 B/3 - No more Asdic transmissions or HE were heard. Considered returning to periscope depth.
0223 B/3 - A pattern of eight depth charges was dropped but not very close. HMS Ultimatum returned to 150 feet and continued withdrawal from the area.
0320 B/3 - Came to periscope depth.
0340 B/3 - Surfaced and resumed the battery charge.

0415 B/3 - Dived in position 43°17'N, 06°59'E and commenced reloading the torpedo tubes and repair the Asdic training gear which had become defective. Reload and repairs were completed one hour later.
2308 B/3 - Surfaced in position 43°18'N, 06°53'E.
2350 B/3, received the Capt S.10's signal timed 2003 B/3 ordering HMS Ultimatum to leave patrol P.M./4. (27)
4 Jul 1944 At 2100 B/4, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) left patrol to comply with the Capt. S.10's signal timed 2003 B/3. (27)
6 Jul 1944 At 0920 B/6, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) ended her 24th war patrol (20th in the Mediterranean) at La Maddalena. (27)

17 Jul 1944 Around 1730 B/17, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed La Maddalena for her 25th war patrol (21st in the Mediterranean). She was ordered to patrol in the Gulf of Lions, in area 'SX'.
She was routes as follows; through position 42°15'N, 07°21'E at 2000 B/18 and 42°15'N, 03°53'E at 1201 B/20 to patrol in area 'SX'.
21 Jul 1944 At 0730 B/21, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) carried out a dummy attack on the Swiss Red Cross ship Albula (1197 GRT, built 1910) which was en-route from Gibraltar to Marseille.
At 1659 B/21, HMS Ultimatum received the Capt. S.10's signal timed 1659 B/21 giving her freedom of action in areas 'SX' and 'SW'. (27)

22 Jul 1944 In a signal timed 0847 B/22, the Capt. S.10 sent the following orders to HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR); Endeavour to intercept Spanish fishing vessel Dot Hermanos believed sailing from Barcelona today to Port Vendres with cargo of Wolfram. Utmost importance to prove this clandestine export. Capture of master should be attempted.

In a followup signal timed 1835 B/22, the following orders were signalled to HMS Ultimatum; (1) My 0847 B/22. Intercept outside territorial waters in Gulf of Rosas halfway between La Escala and Punta Palconera between 1200 B/23 and 1400 B/23. Master of Dos Hermanos is party to this arrangement and will co-operate and capture is not essential. (2) Agents expected to be on board and any suspicious passenger should be captured, any manifest or other evidence of nature of cargo should be removed. Essential to produce unassailable evidence of cargo. Vessel is not to be sunk. Report result by W/T. (92)

23 Jul 1944 In a signal timed 0915 B/23, the Capt. S.10 sent the following orders to HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR); (1) My 1835 B/22. Vessel will not sail from Barcelona until afternoon 24th or 25th. Further signal will be made. (2) Master should not be removed. (3) Bombing restrictions have been arranged. (92)

23 Jul 1944 At 1700 B/23, near Cape Creus, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) carried out a dummy attack on the Swedisch Safe Conduct Ship Finn (1664 GRT, built 1920) bound from Marseille to Gibraltar. (27)

24 Jul 1944 At 1600 B/24, while patrolling near the Gulf of Rosas, Spain, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) sighted a fishing vessel proceeding on course 030°. HMS Ultimatum closed to investigate.

At 1730 B/24, the fishing vessels was identified as the Antonio of Rosas. A small local vessel. Due to the bad visibility it was approach very close before the name of the vessel could be read. Approach was made from ahead and after her identity was established it was seen that she was towing nets. Course was immediately altered away but it appeared HMS Ultimatum fouled her nets. HMS Ultimatum went to 50 feet and retired at 4 knots.
At 2030 B/24, the Capt. S.10's signal timed 1623 B/24 was received which ordered HMS Ultimatum to patrol in area 'SW'. Course was altered to comply with the orders.
At 2207 B/24, HMS Ultimatum surfaced in position 42°11'N, 03°20'E and course was set for the Cassidaigne Reef. (27)
25 Jul 1944 At 0600 B/25, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) entered her new patrol area. (27)

27 Jul 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) fired four torpedoes at a group of small German vessels. The German landing barge F 811 was hit and sunk south of La Ciotat, France in position 43°03'N, 05°34'E.

[We have been unable to identify the other vessels in this convoy (which was numbered 5495). Unfortunately there is no KTB (Kriegstagebuch, logbook) of the German 6th Sicherungsflottille (6th escort flotilla) available after 15 July 1944. This may have been lost during the Allied landings in Southern France in August.]
0050 B/27 - Obtained a radar echo bearing 045°, range 6000 yards. Altered course towards.
0100 B/27 - The bearing was steady, range decreasing, went to action stations.
0117 B/27 - Turbine HE was picked up bearing 050°. Made out dark shapes against the land.
0130 B/27 - The contact was seen to be one escort vessel and four 'F-boats' on an estimated course of 280°, speed 6 knots.
0148 B/27 - Fired three torpedoes at an almost continuous target of 2 'F-boats'. Radar gave a range of 1500 yards.

0150 B/27 - One of the 'F-boats' was seen to explode with a bright yellow flash and a column of sparks and smoke. It was thought one of the other torpedoes hit the other 'F-boat' but this was not the case.
0151 B/27 - The escort was seen to turn towards.
0154 B/27 - An aircraft was seen to fly down the torpedo tracks. Dived and continued withdrawal submerged.
0155 B/27 - Heard breaking up noises.
0247 B/27 - All quiet. Surfaced.
Enemy A/S patrols, mostly aircraft, patrolled the area for the remained of the day. (27)

28 Jul 1944 At 0300 B/28, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) left patrol to comply with the orders in the Capt. S.10's signal timed 1109 B/26. [We have been unable to find the text of this signal but it must have contained the orders to leave patrol and when to arrive at La Maddalena and the route which was to be followed.] (27)
This convoy departed Port Said on 28 July 1944, around 1430 B/28.

On departure from Port Said the convoy was made up of the following merchant vessels; Algorab (Dutch, 4938 GRT, built 1921), Clan MacNeil (British, 6111 GRT, built 1922), Edward Bruce (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Empire Lord (British, 7359 GRT, built 1944), Fort Prudhomme (British, 7167 GRT, built 1943), Gulf of Mexico (American (tanker), 7807 GRT, built 1917), Katy (Norwegian (tanker), 6825 GRT, built 1931), Marsdale (British, 4890 GRT, built 1940), Megna (British, 6595 GRT, built 1944), Nurani (British, 5414 GRT, built 1941), Phrontis (Dutch, 6616 GRT, built 1926), Rookley (British, 4998 GRT, built 1940), Samdak (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Samingoy (British, 7255 GRT, built 1944), Samtruth (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Sarpedon (British, 11321 GRT, built 1923), Suderholm (Norwegian (tanker), 4908 GRT, built 1917) and Thorshavn (Norwegian (tanker), 6869 GRT, built 1930).

The French sloop D'Entrecasteaux (?) also took passage in this convoy. She was not part of the escort though as this ship had been sunk at Diego Suarez in 1942 and had been salvaged in 1943 and made fit to proceed under her own power. She was to proceed to Bizerte where she was placed in reserve. She was never fully repaired.

On departure from Port Said the convoy was escorted by the sloop HMS Pheasant (Cdr. W.E.J. Eames, DSC, RN), the minesweeping sloops Commandant Bory (?), La Moqueuse (?) and the corvette HMS Saxifage (Lt. D.K.H. Kinloch, RNR).

On 29 July 1944, around 1600 B/29, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Alexandria; Bantria (British, 2407 GRT, built 1928), Beaconsfield (British, 4635 GRT, built 1938), City of Dundee (British, 5273 GRT, built 1921), Fort Astoria (British, 7189 GRT, built 1943), Manchester Division (British, 6048 GRT, built 1918), Samfeugh (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) and Woodlark (British, 1501 GRT, built 1928). They were escorted by the sloop HMS Bideford (T/A/Lt.Cdr. H.J. Beverley, DSO, DSC, RNR) and the corvettes HMS Alisma (Lt. G. Lanning, RANVR) and HMS Spiraea (Lt. A.H. Pierce, OBE, RNR) which also joined the convoy.

At 0438 B/3, the following ships parted company with the convoy to proceed to Augusta; Algorab, Bantria, Beaconsfield, Empire Lord, Fort Astoria, Gulf of Mexico, Phrontis, Samdak, Samfeugh, Samingoy, Samtruth and Thorshavn. They were escorted to Augusta by Commandant Bory and La Moqueuse which did not rejoin the convoy.

At 0553 B/3, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Augusta; Arizonan (American, 5816 GRT, built 1920), Baron Maclay (British, 6317 GRT, built 1924), Cartago (American, 4732 GRT, built 1908), Empire Airman (British (tanker), 9813 GRT, built 1942), Empire Gain (British (tanker), 3738 GRT, built 1943), Empire Spey (British, 4292 GRT, built 1929), John Sergeant (American, 7191 GRT, built 1942), Meline (Norwegian (tanker), 6983 GRT, built 1918), Samavon (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943), Sheaf Crown (British, 4868 GRT, built 1929) and Toltec (Honduran, 5527 GRT, built 1929).
At 1110 B/3, the Woodlark parted company with the convoy to proceed to Malta.

At 1600 B/4, the Empire Gain, Empire Spey and Suderholm were detached to Bizerta as was the D'Entrecasteaux. The following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bizerta one hour later; Chloris (British, 1171 GRT, built 1910) and Dallington Court (British, 6889 GRT, built 1929).

At 0940 B/5, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Bone; Antonia (Dutch (tanker), 3357 GRT, built 1938), Clydebank (British, 5156 GRT, built 1925) and Empire Planet (British, 4290 GRT, built 1923).

At 1138 B/6, the following merchant vessels were detached to Algiers; Antonia, Arizonan, Baron Maclay, Chloris, Clydebank, Dallington Court, Empire Airman and Samavon while the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Algiers at 1300 B/6; Astrid (Norwegian, 2861 GRT, built 1942), Henry Baldwin (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), James Hoban (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942), Orpheus (American, 8258 GRT, built 1944), Pierre Soule (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943), Ponce de Leon (American, 7176 GRT, built 1943), Smith Thompson (American, 7176 GRT, built 1942) and William H. Wilmer (American, 7191 GRT, built 1943). Also the submarine HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) joined the convoy coming from Algiers.

At 1350 B/7, the following merchant vessels were detached to Oran; Cartago, Henry Baldwin, James Hoban, John Sergeant, Katy, Orpheus, Ponce de Leon, Smith Thompson, Toltec and William H. Wilmer while the merchant vessel Atlantic (British, 5414 GRT, built 1939) joined the convoy coming from Oran at 1445 B/7.
At 1839 B/8, the following ships were detached to Gibraltar; Astrid, Atlantic, Empire Planet, Megna, Meline and Rookley. Also detached to Gibraltar was HMS Ultimatum.
At 1916 B/8, the Mediterranean escort, HMS Pheasant, HMS Bideford, HMS Alisma, HMS Saxifrage and HMS Spiraea was detached to Gibraltar.

This was after a new escort had joined up with the remaining ships of the convoy which were now proceeding into the Atlantic together with joiners from Gibraltar. The convoy was now known as convoy 'MKS 57(G)'. This convoy was to make rendezvous with and join convoy SL 166 coming from Freetown. These two convoy were then to proceed in company, as the combined convoy SL 166 / MKS 57(G), to the U.K. (93)
30 Jul 1944 Around 0930 B/30, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) ended her 24th war patrol (21st in the Mediterranean) at La Maddalena. (27)
3 Aug 1944 Around 0630 B/3, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed La Maddalena for Algiers. She was to return to the U.K. to refit.
She was routed as follows; through position 41°07'N, 07°34'E at 1830 B/3 thence direct to position 36°56'N, 03°03'E at 1100 B/5 to rendezvous with the escort to Algiers.
For the daily positions of HMS Ultimatum during the passage to the U.K. see the map below.
5 Aug 1944 At 1310 B/5, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Algiers.
She was escorted in by HMS Hoy (T/Lt. G.H. McNair, MBE, RNVR) which had joined at 1115 B/5 in position 36°56'N, 03°03'E. (94)
6 Aug 1944 At 1030 B/6, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Algiers for Gibraltar.
At sea, at 1220 N/6, and in position 36°53'N, 03°05'E, she joined convoy MKS 57.
[For more info on this convoy see the event ' Convoy MKS 57 ' for 28 July 1944.] (94)
8 Aug 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Gibraltar. (94)
9 Aug 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted D/G trials at Gibraltar. (94)
This convoy departed Freetown around 0800 Z/9.

On departure from Freetown the convoy was made up of the following merchant vessels; Allerton (British, 7195 GRT, built 1941), Appledore (British, 5218 GRT, built 1929), Argyll (British, 4897 GRT, built 1939), Baron Elgin (British, 3942 GRT, built 1933), Charles Schiaffino (French, 3664 GRT, built 1930), Clunepark (British, 3491 GRT, built 1928), Domby (British, 5582 GRT, built 1932), Empire Beauty (British, 7297 GRT, built 1943), Empire Guinevere (British, 7072 GRT, built 1942), Fort Glenlyon (British, 7132 GRT, built 1943), Fort Thompson (British, 7134 GRT, built 1942), Fort Wellington (British, 7131 GRT, built 1943), Hoggar (French, 5146 GRT, built 1923), Nailsea Moor (British, 4926 GRT, built 1937), Ocean Vanity (British, 7174 GRT, built 1942), Palembang (Dutch, 7070 GRT, built 1921), Port Darwin (British, 8063 GRT, built 1918), Samdart (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944), Samderwent (British, 7219 GRT, built 1944), Sansu (British, 5446 GRT, built 1939) and Sydland (Swedish, 5134 GRT, built 1920).

On departure from Freetown the convoy was escorted by the frigates HMS Odzani (Lt.Cdr. L.A. Sayers, RD, RNR), HMS Aire (A/Lt.Cdr. N.K. Boyd, DSC and Bar, RNR) and the patrol vessel HMS Kilmington (T/Lt. D.D. Humphreys, RNVR).

At 1100 Z/12, in position 14°15'N, 17°51'W, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Dakar; Bactria (British, 2407 GRT, built 1928), Empire Duchess (British, 7067 GRT, built 1943) and Oued Fes (French, 2554 GRT, built 1913).

At 1000 Z/13, in position 17°33'N, 17°55'W, the Empire Guinevere parted company with the convoy to proceed to Dakar due to engine trouble. HMS Kilmington escorted her to Dakar and then rejoined the convoy.

At 1700 Z/19, in position 33°56'N, 10°30'W, the following merchant vessels joined the convoy coming from Casablanca; Dagrun (Norwegian, 4562 GRT, built 1928) and Empire Planet (British, 4290 GRT, built 1923). They had been escorted to the rendezvous by the patrol vessels L'Emporté and L'Enjoue which subsequently returned to Dakar taking the merchant vessels Charles Schiaffino, Clunepark, Hoggar and Oued Fes with them. They arrived at Casablanca on 20 August.

At 0700 Z/20, in position 35°08'N, 10°16'W, the current escort of the convoy, HMS Odzani, HMS Aire and HMS Kilmington parted company to proceed to Gibraltar. They took the merchant vessel Empire Duchess with them. They arrived at Gibraltar on 21 August.

At 0800 Z/20, convoy MKS 58(G) joined and merged with convoy SL 166 in position 35°08'N, 10°06'W. The following merchant vessels now joined the combined convoy SL 167 / MKS 58(G); Astrid (Norwegian, 2861 GRT, built 1942), Benedick (British (tanker serving as escort oiler), 6978 GRT, built 1928), Boreas (Norwegian, 2801 GRT, built 1920), Capitaine Paul Lemerle (French, 4945 GRT, built 1925), Lagarto (British, 5072 GRT, built 1917), Nolisement (British, 5084 GRT, built 1928), Samshee (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Samthar (British, 7176 GRT, built 1943) and Van de Velde (Dutch, 6389 GRT, built 1919). Also joining were the submarines HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) and HMS Ultor (Lt. G.E. Hunt, DSC and Bar, RN) for onward passage to the U.K.

The escort of concoy MKS 58(G) now formed the escort of the combined convoy SL 167 / MKS 58(G). This escort was made up of the following warships; frigates HMS Bullen (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Parish, RN), HMS Antigua (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) D.F. White, RNR) and the corvettes HMS Allington Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.G. Ogden, DSC, RNVR), HMS Hurst Castle (Lt. H.G. Chesterman, DSC and Bar, RNR), HMS Knaresborough Castle (Lt. J.R. Freeman, RNR), HMS Leeds Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.T. Hodson, DSC, RNVR) and HMS Tintagel Castle (Cdr. G.A. Thring, DSO, RN).
At 1500 Z/20, in position 35°45'N, 11°16'W, the rescue ship Copeland (British, 1526 GRT, built 1923) joined the convoy.

At 1400 A/21, in position 37°11'N, 13°50'W, the escort carrier HMS Activity (Capt. C. Wauchope, DSC, RN) joined the convoy coming from convoy OS 86 / KMS 60(G). She was escorted to the rendezvous with convoy SL 167 / MKS 58(G) by HMS Allington Castle and HMS Leeds Castle which had been detached earlier for this purpose.
At 1730 Z/22, in position 39°49'N, 15°48'W, the merchant vessel Samshire (British, 7219 GRT, built 1943) joined the convoy coming from the Azores.
At 1030 B/27, in position 50°07'N, 05°54'W, the Benedick was detached to convoy OS 87 / KMS 61(G).
At 2215 B/27, in position 51°26'N, 04°30'W, HMS Activity was detached to proceed to Greenock. HMS Antigua went with her as escort.
At 2222 B/27, in position 51°26'N, 04°30'W, the following merchant vessels were detached to Barry Roads; Baron Elgin and Boreas.
At 2258 B/27, in position 51°26'N, 04°41'W, the Nailsea Moor (bound for Loch Ewe for onward routing to a east coast port) was detached to Barry Roads with engine trouble.

At 2000 B/28, in position 53°27'N, 04°48'W, the Mersey and Morecambe Bay sections of the convoy parted company. This were the following merchant vessels; Argyll, Bactria, Empire Beauty, Fort Wellington, Nolisement, Ocean Vanity, Palengbang, Samdart, Samshee, Samshire, Sansu and Sydland.
At 2100 B/28, in position 53°33'N, 04°49'W, HMS Ultimatum and HMS Ultor parted company to proceed to Rothesay and Holy Loch respectively. HMS Bullen went with them as escort.

At 0930 B/29, in position 54°57'N, 05°09'W, the merchant vessel Empire Planet was detached to the Clyde, as was the rescue ship Copeland. The Lagarto (bound for Loch Ewe for onward routing to a east coast port) was also detached to the Clyde due to engine trouble.
The remaining ships of the convoy proceeded to Loch Ewe. (95)
10 Aug 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) is wiped (degaussed) at Gibraltar. (94)
11 Aug 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted compass adjustment trials at Gibraltar. (94)

18 Aug 1944 At 1645 B/18, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) departed Gibraltar for the U.K. She was to join convoy MKS 58(G) together with HMS Ultor (Lt. G.E. Hunt, DSC and Bar, RN). This convoy was later to merge with convoy SL 167 forming the combined convoy SL 167 / MKS 58(G).
[For more info on convoy MKS 58(G) see the event ' Convoy MKS 58(G) ' for 18 August 1944.]
[For more info on the combined convoy SL 167 / MKS 58(G) see the event ' Convoy SL 167 / Combined convoy SL 167 / KMS 58(G) ' for 9 August 1944.] (27)
The remaining merchant vessels of convoy MKS 58 passed Gibraltar into the Atlantic in the evening of 18 August 1944.

This were the following merchant vessels; Capitaine Paul Lemerle (French, 4945 GRT, built 1925), Lagarto (British, 5072 GRT, built 1917), Samshee (British, 7210 GRT, built 1944), Samthar (British, 7176 GRT, built 1943) and Van de Velde (Dutch, 6389 GRT, built 1919).

The new escort for convoy MKS 58(G) had joined the convoy at 2000 A/18 relieving the Mediterranean escort which then parted company to proceed to Gibraltar. The new escort was made up of the following vessels; Frigates HMS Bullen (Lt.Cdr. A.H. Parish, RN), HMS Antigua (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) D.F. White, RNR) and the corvettes HMS Allington Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.G. Ogden, DSC, RNVR), HMS Hurst Castle (Lt. H.G. Chesterman, DSC and Bar, RNR), HMS Knaresborough Castle (Lt. J.R. Freeman, RNR), HMS Leeds Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W.T. Hodson, DSC, RNVR) and HMS Tintagel Castle (Lt. R. Atkinson, DSC and Bar, RNR).

With these new escort vessels the following merchant vessels also joined the convoy; Astrid (Norwegian, 2861 GRT, built 1942), Benedick (British (tanker serving as escort oiler), 6978 GRT, built 1928), Boreas (Norwegian, 2801 GRT, built 1920) and Nolisement (British, 5084 GRT, built 1928). The submarines HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) and HMS Ultor (Lt. G.E. Hunt, DSC and Bar, RN) also joined the convoy for onward passage to the U.K.
At 0800 Z/20, the convoy made rendezvous with convoy SL 167 coming from Freetown and the combined convoy SL 167 / MKS 58(G) was formed. (93)
29 Aug 1944 Around 1115 B/29, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Rothesay from Gibraltar.
It was not possible to take HMS Ultimatum in hand for refit before the end of October. Later this was even further postponed until mid-December. (27)
27 Sep 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted D/G trials on the D/G range off Helensburgh. (96)
29 Sep 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) is wiped at Greenock following which she conducted D/G trials on the D/G range off Helensburgh. (96)

1 Oct 1944 Around 1350 A/1, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) departed Rothesay for Plymouth. She made the passage together with HMS Tiptoe (Lt.Cdr. P.R.H. Harrison, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) and HMS Unshaken (Lt. J.S. Pearce, RNR) (until Fishguard). They were escorted by HMS Kihna (Cdr.(Retd.) T.J.T.C. Jenks, RN). (97)
3 Oct 1944 Around 1155 A/3, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Plymouth. (97)

4 Oct 1944 Around 0700 A/4, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) and HMS Tiptoe (Lt.Cdr. P.R.H. Harrison, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) departed from Plymouth for Portland where they arrived around 1745 A/4. They were escorted by HMS Cotillion (T/Lt. H.J. Howard, RNR). (97)

5 Oct 1944 Around 0840 A/5, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) and HMS Tiptoe (Lt.Cdr. P.R.H. Harrison, DSO, DSC and Bar, RN) proceeded from Portland to Portsmouth (Gosport / Fort Blockhouse) where they arrived around 1610 A/5. They were escorted by HMS Cotillion (T/Lt. H.J. Howard, RNR). (97)
11 Oct 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (97)
12 Oct 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (97)
17 Oct 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (97)
19 Oct 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (97)
24 Oct 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (97)
26 Oct 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (97)
1 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
2 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
6 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
7 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
8 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) is docked in AFD 2 at Gosport. (98)
9 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) was undocked. (98)
10 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
11 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted noise trials off Portsmouth. (98)
14 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
17 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted exercises off Portsmouth. (98)
20 Nov 1944 Around 0800 A/20, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Gosport (Portsmouth) for Fishguard. She was escorted by HMS Darthema (T/Lt. C. Brunning, RNVR). (98)
22 Nov 1944 At 1420 A/22, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Fishguard from Gosport (Portsmouth). (98)
26 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted noise trials off Fishguard. (98)
27 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted special trials off Fishguard. (98)
29 Nov 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted special trials off Fishguard. (98)
4 Dec 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard with HMS Fitzroy (A/Lt.Cdr. A.J. McCullogh Miller, DSC, RNVR). (99)
5 Dec 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted special trials off Fishguard with HMS Fitzroy (A/Lt.Cdr. A.J. McCullogh Miller, DSC, RNVR). (99)
7 Dec 1944 At 0555 A/7, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Fishguard for Portland. She was escorted by HMS Mignonette (Lt. H.H. Brown, DSC, RNR). (99)
8 Dec 1944 Around 1900 A/8, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Portland from Fishguard. (99)
9 Dec 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted special D/G trials in Portland Bay. (99)
10 Dec 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) conducted special D/G trials in Portland Bay. (99)

11 Dec 1944 Around 0800 A/11, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Portland for Gosport (Portsmouth) where she arrived around 1505 A/11. She was escorted by HMS Brontes (Skr. A.E. Wood, RNR). (99)
15 Dec 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) participated in special trials off Portsmouth with HMMTB 221 (T/Lt. R.M. Walters, RNVR). (99)
21 Dec 1944 At 1630 A/21, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) departed Gosport (Portsmouth) for Sheerness. (99)
22 Dec 1944 Around 1400 A/22, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) arrived at Sheerness from Gosport (Postsmouth). (99)
23 Dec 1944 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, DSC, RNR) proceeded from Sheerness to Tilbury where she was to refit at the Green and Silley Weir Ltd. Shipyard. (99)
14 Apr 1945 With her refit completed, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) proceeded from Tilbury to Sheerness. (100)
15 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) conducted compass adjustment and D/G trials off Sheerness. (100)
16 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) proceeded from Sheerness to the Chatham Dockyard to embark ammunition. (100)
17 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) proceeded from the Chatham Dockyard to Sheerness. (100)
18 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) conducted post refit trials off Sheerness. (100)
19 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) conducted post refit trials off Sheerness. (100)
21 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) proceeded from Sheerness to Tilbury as some problems had been encountered with the generators during the post refit trials. (100)
25 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) conducted engine trials off Tilbury. (100)
27 Apr 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) proceeded from Tilbury to Sheerness. (100)

29 Apr 1945 Around 0650 B/29, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) departed Sheerness for Dundee. She made the passage together with HMS Surf (Lt. H.W. Wilkinson, RN) departed Sheerness for Dundee. They were escorted by HMS Kingston Olivine (Lt. E.P. Evers, RANVR). (100)
1 May 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. P.D.C. Bennett, RN) arrived at Dundee from Sheerness.
HMS Ultimatum was assigned to training duties mainly based at Blyth.
[No logs are available for the months of May, June, July and August 1945 so no details can be given on these training duties.] (90)
24 Aug 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. J.O. Coote, RN) departed Blyth for Gosport (Portsmouth). (90)
26 Aug 1945 HMS Ultimatum (Lt. J.O. Coote, RN) arrived at Gosport (Portsmouth) from Blyth. (90)
